# Session Learnings (Append-Only Log)

> Each entry records a non-obvious insight from an lmv-autodev or adversarial session. Agents consult this to avoid repeating mistakes and to build on prior discoveries.

---

## 2026-04-11 — Adversarial Loop Run 1

**Session:** First live test of `lmv adversarial-loop`
**Key insight:** 100% failure rate on generated pipelines — the adversarial generator targeting known weak spots is extremely effective at finding bugs. Every single pipeline generated by Opus 4.6 with `nested_expressions,math_on_params,foreach_expression_items,complex_conditions` triggered at least one known failure.

**Specific findings:**
- W-9 (`copy_sql_reader_query_silent_placeholder`) hit 3 times — Opus naturally generates Copy activities with SQL queries containing expressions
- W-10 (`for_each_items_silent_placeholder`) hit 2 times — ForEach with `createArray()` items is a natural pattern Opus produces
- `activity_coverage` was consistently the worst dimension — many generated activities are types that wkmigrate doesn't fully support yet

**Cost observation:** Each pipeline generation takes ~30-60s with Opus 4.6 (token-heavy prompts + responses). At 10 pipelines/round, each round takes ~160s. Budget accordingly.

**Actionable for next session:**
- Add `"unsupported_types"` to weak_spots to specifically target the activity_coverage gap
- Consider using Sonnet for generation (faster, cheaper) when the goal is volume over depth
- The convergence_patience=3 is too generous — set to 2 for quick runs since the same 2 clusters dominate

---

## 2026-04-11 — L-F19 Detector Discovery

**Session:** D/C/A/B task session
**Key insight:** The W-9 finding (Copy sql_reader_query dropped) was only visible AFTER the W-8 fixture fix unblocked the Copy translation path. Findings can be chained — fixing one issue unblocks discovery of the next.

**Specific findings:**
- wkmigrate's `_parse_sql_format_options` builds CopyActivity.source_properties from a fixed key list
- The key list does NOT include `sql_reader_query` — the query is silently lost
- L-F19 detector (`_detect_dropped_expression_fields`) now catches this at translate time

**Actionable for next session:**
- After every wkmigrate fix lands, re-run the adversarial loop — new fixes may reveal previously-masked bugs
- The L-F19 detector pattern (walk the IR for fields that should exist but don't) is reusable for other activities

---

## 2026-04-11 — Branch Strategy Decision

**Session:** Architectural question about alpha_1 vs pr/27-N
**Key insight:** Never merge two parallel implementations of the same feature. The alpha_1 branch independently implemented the feature/27-phase* work AND the pr/27-N rewrite exists — merging them creates 17 conflicts from genuinely different code, not just formatting.

**Specific findings:**
- alpha_1 has emission_config, strategy_router, H1 fix, expression resolver redesign — NONE of this exists on pr/27-N
- pr/27-N is cleaner and more focused — chosen as canonical
- alpha_1 needs REBUILD (cherry-pick valuable commits onto pr/27-4), not merge

**Actionable for next session:**
- When /wkmigrate-autodev runs for pr/27-N, use `dev/wkmigrate-alpha1-replay-handoff.md` as input
- The 10 commits to port are classified as Group A (emission), B (resolver), C (bugfix)
